RANGOON About Kachin civilians Kachin State's Hpakant Township have been newly displaced clashes between Burma Army Kachin Independence Army (KIA) recent days, according local leaders Kachin rebel sources, said that fighting erupted after government forces entered rebel-controlled ruby mining area.
San, National League Democracy (NLD) member based Hpakant town, said visited civilians fled Para village were provided refuge churches another village located east Hpakant.

church about people another church over people.
They away from their homes they were afraid fighting," said, adding that other Kachin leaders from Hpakant delivered food displaced villagers Tuesday.
Daung Khar, member technical consultancy team Kachin Independence Organization, political wing KIA, said rebels clashed with Burma Army last Friday Sunday Sabaw Maw, ruby mine controlled KIA.
"According news received, government troops entered area control.
This troops shoot them," said, adding that lacked further details fighting.
Kachin News Group reported that four Burmese soldiers were killed during violence that government troops seized base next ruby mine burned down.
been fighting insurgency against Burma Army since mid-2011 when 17-year-old ceasefire broke down.
Since then, some 120,000 civilians Kachin northern Shan states have been displaced.
Hostilities quieted down February 2013, skirmishes have continued bilateral ceasefire between sides proven elusive.
recent months, fighting northern Burma intensified several thousand local civilians have been newly displaced.
Fighting also spread northern Shan State government troops have increasingly fought with Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA), ally that also lacks ceasefire with Naypyidaw.
